heading: Source water petition program
label: 300j–14 Source water petition program

children.label: i
children.text: reduce the presence in drinking water of contaminants that may be addressed by a petition by considering the origins of the contaminants, including to the maximum extent practicable the specific activities that affect the drinking water supply of a community;

children.label: ii
children.text: obtain financial or technical assistance necessary to facilitate establishment of a partnership, or to develop and implement recommendations of a partnership for the protection of source water to assist in the provision of drinking water that complies with national primary drinking water regulations with respect to contaminants addressed by a petition; and

children.label: iii
children.text: develop recommendations regarding voluntary and incentive-based strategies for the long-term protection of the source water of community water systems.

children.label: i
children.text: use funds set aside pursuant to by the State to carry out a program described in subparagraph (A), including assistance to voluntary local partnerships for the development and implementation of partnership recommendations for the protection of source water such as source water quality assessment, contingency plans, and demonstration projects for partners within a source water area delineated under ; andsection 300j–12(k)(1)(A)(iii) of this titlesection 300j–13(a) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: provide assistance in response to a petition submitted under this subsection using funds referred to in subsection (b)(2)(B).

children.label: A
children.text: facilitate the local development of voluntary, incentive-based partnerships among owners and operators of community water systems, governments, and other persons in source water areas; and

children.label: B
children.text: obtain assistance from the State in identifying resources which are available to implement the recommendations of the partnerships to address the origins of drinking water contaminants that may be addressed by a petition (including to the maximum extent practicable the specific activities contributing to the presence of the contaminants) that affect the drinking water supply of a community.

children.label: A
children.text: that are pathogenic organisms for which a national primary drinking water regulation has been established or is required under ; orsection 300g–1 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: above the maximum contaminant level; or

children.label: ii
children.text: that are not reliably and consistently below the maximum contaminant level.

children.label: A
children.text: include a delineation of the source water area in the State that is the subject of the petition;

children.label: B
children.text: identify, to the maximum extent practicable, the origins of the drinking water contaminants that may be addressed by a petition (including to the maximum extent practicable the specific activities contributing to the presence of the contaminants) in the source water area delineated under ;section 300j–13 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: identify any deficiencies in information that will impair the development of recommendations by the voluntary local partnership to address drinking water contaminants that may be addressed by a petition;

children.label: i
children.text: the municipal or local government or other political subdivision of the State (including a county that is designated by the State to act on behalf of an unincorporated area within that county) with jurisdiction over the source water area delineated under ; andsection 300j–13 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: who is likely to be affected by recommendations of the voluntary local partnership; and

children.label: II
children.text: whose participation is essential to the success of the partnership;

children.label: E
children.text: outline how the voluntary local partnership has or will, during development and implementation of recommendations of the voluntary local partnership, identify, recognize and take into account any voluntary or other activities already being undertaken by persons in the source water area delineated under under Federal or State law to reduce the likelihood that contaminants will occur in drinking water at levels of public health concern; andsection 300j–13 of this title

children.label: F
children.text: specify the technical, financial, or other assistance that the voluntary local partnership requests of the State to develop the partnership or to implement recommendations of the partnership.

children.heading: Savings provision
children.label: 5 Savings provision
children.text: Unless otherwise provided within the agreement, an agreement between an unincorporated area and a county for the county to submit a petition under paragraph (1)(A) on behalf of the unincorporated area shall not authorize the county to act on behalf of the unincorporated area in any matter not within a program under this section.

children.heading: Approval or disapproval of petitions
children.label: b Approval or disapproval of petitions

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: After providing notice and an opportunity for public comment on a petition submitted under subsection (a), the State shall approve or disapprove the petition, in whole or in part, not later than 120 days after the date of submission of the petition.

children.label: i
children.text: the relative priority of the public health concern identified in the petition with respect to the other water quality needs identified by the State;

children.label: ii
children.text: any necessary coordination that the State will perform of the program established under this section with programs implemented or planned by other States under this section; and

children.label: iii
children.text: funds available (including funds available from a State revolving loan fund established under title VI of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act ( et seq.)) or ;33 U.S.C. 1381section 300j–12 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: any program established under the Federal Water Pollution Control Act ( et seq.);33 U.S.C. 1251

children.label: ii
children.text: the program established under ;section 1455b of title 16

children.label: iii
children.text: the agricultural water quality protection program established under chapter 2 of subtitle D of title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.);16 U.S.C. 38381See References in Text note below.1

children.label: iv
children.text: the sole source aquifer protection program established under ;section 300h–6 of this title

children.label: v
children.text: the community wellhead protection program established under ;section 300h–7 of this title

children.label: vi
children.text: any pesticide or ground water management plan;

children.label: vii
children.text: any voluntary agricultural resource management plan or voluntary whole farm or whole ranch management plan developed and implemented under a process established by the Secretary of Agriculture; and

children.label: viii
children.text: any abandoned well closure program; and

children.label: C
children.text: a description of activities that will be undertaken to coordinate Federal and State programs to respond to the petition.

children.label: A
children.text: new information becomes available;

children.label: B
children.text: conditions affecting the source water that is the subject of the petition change; or

children.label: C
children.text: modifications are made in the type of assistance being requested.

children.heading: Grants to support State programs
children.label: c Grants to support State programs

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator may make a grant to each State that establishes a program under this section that is approved under paragraph (2). The amount of each grant shall not exceed 50 percent of the cost of administering the program for the year in which the grant is available.

children.heading: Approval
children.label: 2 Approval
children.text: In order to receive grant assistance under this subsection, a State shall submit to the Administrator for approval a plan for a source water quality protection partnership program that is consistent with the guidance published under subsection (d). The Administrator shall approve the plan if the plan is consistent with the guidance published under subsection (d).

children.label: A
children.text: States in the development of a source water quality protection partnership program; and

children.label: B
children.text: municipal or local governments or political subdivisions of a State and community water systems in the development of source water quality protection partnerships and in the assessment of source water quality.

children.label: A
children.text: recommend procedures for the approval or disapproval by a State of a petition submitted under subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: recommend procedures for the submission of petitions developed under subsection (a);

children.label: C
children.text: recommend criteria for the assessment of source water areas within a State; and

children.label: D
children.text: describe technical or financial assistance pursuant to Federal and State programs that is available to address the contamination of sources of drinking water and to develop and respond to petitions submitted under subsection (a).

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: e Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $5,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 2020 through 2026. Each State with a plan for a program approved under subsection (b) shall receive an equitable portion of the funds available for any fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: creates or conveys new authority to a State, political subdivision of a State, or community water system for any new regulatory measure; or

children.label: B
children.text: limits any authority of a State, political subdivision, or community water system; or

precludes a community water system, municipal or local government, or political subdivision of a government from locally developing and carrying out a voluntary, incentive-based, source water quality protection partnership to address the origins of drinking water contaminants of public health concern.
